question: How do you ask 'Where is the bathroom?' in Ukrainian?
incorrectAnswers: {{uk-UA|Що таке туалет?|Shcho take tualet?|SHCHO TA-ke tua-LET?}}
explanation: 'De' means 'where'. 'De tualet?' is the most practical travel phrase.

question: How do you ask 'How much does this cost?' in Ukrainian?
correctAnswer: {{uk-UA|Скільки це коштує?|Skilky tse koshtuye?|SKIL-ky TSE KOSH-tuye?}}
incorrectAnswers: {{uk-UA|Що це таке?|Shcho tse take?|SHCHO TSE TA-ke?}}
{{uk-UA|Де це купити?|De tse kupyty?|DE TSE ku-PY-ty?}}
{{uk-UA|Чи є знижка?|Chy ye znyzhka?|CHY YE ZNYZH-ka?}}
explanation: 'Skilky tse koshtuye?' — 'How much does this cost?' — essential for markets.

question: How do you order 'a coffee, please' in Ukrainian?
correctAnswer: {{uk-UA|Каву, будь ласка.|Kavu, bud laska.|KA-vu, BUD LAS-ka.}}
explanation: '[Item], bud laska' — '[Item], please' — the polite café order pattern.

question: How do you ask for the bill in Ukrainian?
correctAnswer: {{uk-UA|Рахунок, будь ласка.|Rakhunok, bud laska.|rak-HU-nok, BUD LAS-ka.}}
incorrectAnswers: {{uk-UA|Меню, будь ласка.|Menyu, bud laska.|ME-nyu, BUD LAS-ka.}}
{{uk-UA|Ще одну страву.|Shche odnu stravu.|SHCHE OD-nu STRA-vu.}}
{{uk-UA|Дякую за їжу.|Dyakuyu za yizhu.|DYA-kuyu ZA YIZ-hu.}}
explanation: 'Rakhunok' = the bill/check. Add 'bud laska' to make it polite.

question: How do you say 'I don't understand' in Ukrainian?
correctAnswer: {{uk-UA|Я не розумію.|Ya ne rozumiyu.|YA NE ro-ZU-miyu.}}
incorrectAnswers: {{uk-UA|Я не знаю.|Ya ne znayu.|YA NE ZNAYU.}}
{{uk-UA|Я не чую.|Ya ne chuyu.|YA NE CHUYU.}}
{{uk-UA|Я не говорю.|Ya ne hovoryu.|YA NE ho-VO-ryu.}}
explanation: 'Ya ne rozumiyu' — the essential phrase for when you've lost the thread.

question: How do you say 'Do you speak English?' in Ukrainian?
correctAnswer: {{uk-UA|Ви говорите англійською?|Vy hovoryte angliyiyskoyu?|VY ho-vo-RY-te an-GLIYIYS-koyu?}}
incorrectAnswers: {{uk-UA|Ви розумієте мене?|Vy rozumiyete mene?|VY ro-zu-MIYE-te ME-ne?}}
{{uk-UA|Ви знаєте англійську?|Vy znayete angliysku?|VY ZNAYE-te an-GLIYS-ku?}}
explanation: Polite plural form 'Vy hovoryte angliyiyskoyu?' — informal: 'Ty hovorysh angliyiyskoyu?'

question: How do you say 'hello' in Ukrainian?
explanation: 'Pryvit' is the casual hello. Formal: 'Dobroho dnya' (good day).

question: How do you say 'thank you' in Ukrainian?
explanation: 'Dyakuyu' is the everyday thank you. 'Dyakuyu duzhe' = thank you very much.

question: How do you say 'please' in Ukrainian?
explanation: 'Bud laska' means please and is also used for 'you're welcome' and 'here you go'.

question: How do you say 'yes' and 'no' in Ukrainian?
explanation: 'Tak' is yes, 'Ni' is no. Note: Ukrainian 'Tak' and Russian 'Da' differ.

question: How do you say 'goodbye' in Ukrainian?
explanation: 'Do pobachennya' — 'until we see each other' — is the formal goodbye. Informal: 'Buvay'.

question: How do you say 'my name is' in Ukrainian?
incorrectAnswers: {{uk-UA|Я живу в...|Ya zhyvu v...|YA ZHY-vu V...}}
explanation: 'Mene zvaty [name]' — 'My name is [name]' — the standard self-introduction.

question: How do you say 'water' in Ukrainian?
explanation: 'Voda' means water — same root as Russian. 'Khlib' is bread, 'moloko' is milk.

question: How do you say 'excuse me / sorry' in Ukrainian?
explanation: 'Vybachte' is formal excuse me/sorry. Informal: 'Vybachay'. Used to get attention or apologize.

question: How do you say 'Help!' in Ukrainian?
incorrectAnswers: {{uk-UA|Я не розумію.|Ya ne rozumiyu.|YA NE ro-zu-MI-yu.}}
explanation: Use 'Допоможіть!' when you urgently need help in a public place.

question: How do you ask for one ticket in Ukrainian?
correctAnswer: {{uk-UA|Один квиток, будь ласка.|Odyn kvytok, bud laska.|o-DYN kvi-TOK, BUD LAS-ka.}}
incorrectAnswers: {{uk-UA|Одне таксі, будь ласка.|Odne taksi, bud laska.|OD-ne tak-SI, BUD LAS-ka.}}
{{uk-UA|Один рахунок, будь ласка.|Odyn rakhunok, bud laska.|o-DYN ra-KHU-nok, BUD LAS-ka.}}
{{uk-UA|Одна кава, будь ласка.|Odna kava, bud laska.|od-NA KA-va, BUD LAS-ka.}}
explanation: This is the core ticket phrase for buses and trains; add the destination after it when needed.

question: How do you ask 'Where is the train or bus to ___?' in Ukrainian?
correctAnswer: {{uk-UA|Де автобус або потяг до _____?|De avtobus abo potyah do _____?|DE av-TO-bus A-bo po-TYAH do _____?}}
incorrectAnswers: {{uk-UA|Куди їде цей автобус?|Kudy yide tsey avtobus?|ku-DY YI-de tsey av-TO-bus?}}
{{uk-UA|Я хочу автобус до _____.|Ya khochu avtobus do _____.|YA KHO-chu av-TO-bus do _____ .}}
{{uk-UA|Автобус тут зупиняється?|Avtobus tut zupynyayetsya?|av-TO-bus tut zu-py-NYA-yet-sya?}}
explanation: This is a practical station question when you need the platform or departure point.

question: How do you call for a taxi in Ukrainian?
explanation: A short direct call that is still useful on the street or in front of a station.

question: How do you say 'Take me there, please' in Ukrainian?
correctAnswer: {{uk-UA|Відвезіть мене туди, будь ласка.|Vidvezit mene tudy, bud laska.|vid-ve-ZIT me-NE tu-DY, BUD LAS-ka.}}
incorrectAnswers: {{uk-UA|Покажіть мені це місце на мапі.|Pokazhit meni tse mistse na mapi.|po-ka-ZHIT me-NI tse MIS-tse na MA-pi.}}
{{uk-UA|Я зупинюся тут.|Ya zupynyusya tut.|YA zu-py-NYU-sya tut.}}
{{uk-UA|Я хочу виписатись.|Ya khochu vypysatys.|YA KHO-chu vy-py-SA-tys.}}
explanation: This is the convenient taxi phrase when you can point to a map, sign, or address.

question: How do you ask 'Do you have any rooms available?' in Ukrainian?
correctAnswer: {{uk-UA|У вас є вільні кімнати?|U vas ye vilni kimnaty?|U VAS YE VIL-ni kim-NA-ty?}}
incorrectAnswers: {{uk-UA|Скільки коштує кімната?|Skilky koshtuye kimnata?|SKIL-ky kosh-TU-ye kim-NA-ta?}}
{{uk-UA|Можу я подивитись кімнату?|Mozhu ya podyvytys kimnatu?|MO-zhu YA po-dy-VY-tys kim-NA-tu?}}
{{uk-UA|Я хочу виписатись.|Ya khochu vypysatys.|YA KHO-chu vy-py-SA-tys.}}
explanation: This is the quickest front-desk question when you still need a place to stay.
